climate.description: Cambria mornings can bring dense coastal fog, as documented in contemporary rider footage of the Old Creek Road climb.
challenges: Early (5am) starts in coastal fog with very limited visibility on the first major climb

terrain.primary: Mixed pavement and dirt — Central Coast coastal/inland
terrain.surface: Mixed: paved Highway 1 coastal sections plus unpaved climbs (Santa Rita Creek Road, Kiler Canyon); roughly 20-30% unpaved on the archived Heroic route (estimate), with a verified 78% paved/22% gravel split on the shorter Santa Lucia route
features: Highway 1 coastal riding, Cambria to Cayucos
Cypress Mountain (mixed gravel/paved climb and descent via Santa Rosa Road — own retained rider footage shows a gravel descent start transitioning to pavement lower down)
Halter Ranch, Cass Winery, and Olea Farm stops

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 66. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 61. EDITORIAL WAVE 8 (2026-07-24): no dimension changes made — this event is now confirmed defunct (last held 2023), so the score is retained as a historical rating of the event as it existed rather than rescored, consistent with how birkebeinerrittet-road.json handles a defunct event. See catalog_flags for a taxonomy caveat matching eroica-dolomiti.json's precedent: fondo_rating.discipline is 'sportive' but the actual event had substantial unpaved content (~20-30% estimated on the Heroic route, verified 22% gravel on the Santa Lucia route) and vintage-bike equipment rules — it doesn't cleanly fit a paved-sportive taxonomy, and road_surface=1/descent_technicality=4 should be read with that in mind.

key_climbs.name: Old Creek Road
key_climbs.note: Paved inland climb, on some Cambria route versions; contemporary rider footage documents it in dense coastal fog

key_climbs.name: Santa Rita Creek Road
key_climbs.note: Gravel climb, roughly 4-5 miles at an estimated ~4.5% average with pitches around 9%

key_climbs.name: Kiler Canyon
key_climbs.note: Narrow, steep dirt/gravel climb, frequently walked by riders

key_climbs.name: Cypress Mountain
key_climbs.note: Mixed gravel/paved climb with sections exceeding 10%; the Santa Rosa Road descent starts on gravel before transitioning to pavement (per contemporary rider footage), not a purely paved climb as this profile previously stated

climb_profile.profile_type: Mixed paved/unpaved Central Coast climbing — no single summit, several distinct climbs combining steep dirt (Kiler Canyon, Santa Rita Creek Road, and the Cypress Mountain/Santa Rosa Road gravel descent) with paved ramps (Old Creek Road)

course_description.character: Eroica California (also called L'Eroica California) was a vintage-bicycle heritage ride based in Cambria on California's Central Coast — mixed paved and unpaved terrain, not the purely paved sportive this profile previously described. The final-era Heroic route ran approximately 107.3 miles/172.7km with roughly 20-30% unpaved sections (an estimate; no official surface breakdown was published for that specific route). It combined paved Highway 1 coastal riding between Cambria and Cayucos with genuine dirt climbing on Santa Rita Creek Road and Kiler Canyon, plus the paved Old Creek Road climb and the mixed gravel/paved Cypress Mountain climb and Santa Rosa Road descent. A shorter Santa Lucia route had a verified breakdown of 56 paved miles plus roughly 16 gravel miles (about 78% paved/22% gravel). Wine-country stops at Halter Ranch, Cass Winery, and Olea Farm were regular features. Riders on the vintage Classic used pre-1987 (or period-style) steel bicycles with downtube shifters and toe clips, per the general Eroica standard — though California's on-course enforcement of those rules was reportedly looser than the written standard, per participant testimony. A separate, co-located modern-bike ride, NOVA Eroica California (launched 2017), ran on Saturdays with road/gravel/cyclocross bikes and timed sectors; it never replaced the vintage Classic — both ran the same weekend through 2023. The event was last held September 15-17, 2023, and has not returned since. This profile now describes a discontinued event, not a bookable one.
course_description.signature_challenge: Cypress Mountain — roughly 4.2 miles climbing to 1,083 feet with sections over 10%, followed by the long Santa Rosa Road descent that starts on gravel before transitioning to pavement — was the course's defining mixed-surface test, alongside Santa Rita Creek Road and Kiler Canyon.
suffering_zones: Santa Rita Creek Road — roughly 4-5 miles of gravel climbing averaging an estimated 4.5% with pitches around 9%
Kiler Canyon — narrow, steep dirt/gravel that riders frequently walked
Cypress Mountain — approximately 4.2 miles and 1,083 feet with sections exceeding 10%, into the long Santa Rosa Road descent that begins on gravel before transitioning to pavement

logistics.lodging_strategy: Cambria and Paso Robles offered hotels, vacation rentals, and campgrounds during the event's run; it was part of a multi-day festival weekend alongside the co-located, modern-bike NOVA Eroica California ride.

history.origin_story: Founded in 2015 as the California edition of the L'Eroica vintage-cycling family, with an inaugural ride on April 12, 2015 in Paso Robles (Eroica's own history page currently states 2016, but contemporary 2015 evidence — including Bianchi's 2015 event calendar — is more authoritative). Later editions were based in Cambria. Operated by Eroica USA Ride & Festival (recorded as an LLC in county contracts, an Inc. in Eroica's own privacy policy — the corporate suffix is inconsistent across official records). Wesley Hatakeyama was the inaugural organizer; Amedeo Polito headed the event in its final years.
notable_moments: 2017: NOVA Eroica California launched as a co-located, modern-bike (road/gravel/cyclocross) gravel-fondo companion to the vintage Classic — a genuine Eroica-family spinoff, not a rebrand of the Classic.
2020: postponed from its original April date due to COVID-19; ultimately cancelled for both 2020 and 2021.
2022: returned April 30-May 1; a federal BLM report confirms hundreds of vintage cyclists used the Piedras Blancas Light Station on course.

2022: Cambria Tourism Board minutes cite operational strain — the event coordinator left before the event, the event was reportedly not profitable, and Italy-based Eroica management created time-zone coordination friction; these are credible contributing circumstances, not a formally confirmed cause of the eventual shutdown.
2023: final verified edition, held September 15-17 (7th edition) — NOVA on Saturday, the vintage Classic on Sunday.
2024: the planned edition was cancelled; surviving organizer text says only the event 'will not be held this year,' with no reason given.

2025-2026: no Eroica California edition has run; the event does not appear on Eroica's official global calendar. An independent, unaffiliated ride — Central Coast Classico — launched in the same region in April 2026 as a de facto local successor, though it is not branded or operated as an Eroica event.
history.reputation: A real but ultimately unsustainable regional L'Eroica outpost — genuine vintage-cycling heritage and real Central Coast terrain, undercut by COVID interruption and reported profitability/coordination strain that preceded its quiet 2024 cancellation and non-return.

biased_opinion.summary: There is no current Eroica California to rate. The event's last verified edition ran September 15-17, 2023 — its 7th year — after COVID cancelled the 2020 and 2021 editions and the event returned for 2022 and 2023. The planned 2024 edition was cancelled outright with no reason given, and no edition has run since; California does not appear on Eroica's official global calendar for 2025 or 2026. Cambria Tourism Board minutes from 2022 point to real operational strain behind the scenes — an event coordinator who left before the event, reported unprofitability, and time-zone coordination friction with Italy-based Eroica management — credible contributing circumstances, though no source formally confirms them as the reason for the final shutdown. When it ran, it was a genuine vintage-bicycle heritage ride with real Central Coast dirt (Santa Rita Creek Road, Kiler Canyon) alongside paved Highway 1 and inland climbs, plus a co-located modern-bike NOVA Eroica ride — a real event with real terrain, not a marketing shell. An independent, unaffiliated ride called Central Coast Classico launched in the same region in April 2026, but it is not an Eroica-branded event and should not be treated as this event's continuation.
strengths: When it ran, genuine vintage-cycling heritage-ride terrain — real dirt on Santa Rita Creek Road and Kiler Canyon, not just a paved sportive with a costume theme
Distinctive wine-country stops at Halter Ranch and Cass Winery integrated into the route
A real multi-year track record (2015-2023, with a 2020-2021 COVID gap) rather than a one-off event
Co-located NOVA Eroica California gave modern-bike riders a legitimate parallel option on the same weekend
weaknesses: This event does not currently exist — it should not be presented to riders as a bookable ride
Even during its run, on-course rule enforcement of vintage-bike standards was reportedly looser than the written Eroica standard, per participant testimony

No reliable official field-size or elevation figure exists for the final-era course — the prior '2,000-rider limit' and '7,500ft' figures on this profile were not well-sourced and may reflect cross-route contamination from other Eroica or NOVA route descriptions
Real operational strain (unprofitability, coordinator turnover, cross-timezone management friction with Italy) preceded the quiet 2024 cancellation — this was not simply a COVID casualty
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Retired as of 2023. If you want a Central Coast vintage-bike or gravel-heritage ride today, look at the independent Central Coast Classico — a genuine but unaffiliated local successor, not an Eroica-branded continuation.

final_verdict.one_liner: California's L'Eroica outpost — genuine Central Coast dirt and wine-country stops, retired after 2023.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — this event does not currently exist. If you want the experience it offered, look at the independent Central Coast Classico, which picked up similar Cambria-area terrain in 2026 without the Eroica branding.
final_verdict.alternatives: Central Coast Classico (unaffiliated local successor, Cambria area) for similar terrain; Eroica Dolomiti or the original L'Eroica (Tuscany) for a currently-active vintage-bike heritage ride within the actual Eroica family.

youtube_data.search_query: Eroica California gran fondo cycling Cambria

videos.title: Eroica California 2019 A Vintage Bicycle Festival
videos.description: The L'Eroica is a weekend long celebration of cycling history that takes place each year on the Central Coast of California. This year the ride started in Cambria, a bucolic town located North of San Luis Obispo and South of Big Sur. The area is most well known for it's beautiful undeveloped coast line, a perfect place to ride bicycles. This year we ride the Heroic route, a 107 mile mixed terrain ride through the farms, vineyards, and coastal mountains. The ride starts at 5:00 AM and rolls South on Hwy 1 before climbing Santa Rita Creek road. Many participating wineries and businesses provide snacks and beverages along the route. Come along as we meet new and well known friends, attend the concourse bike show, and ride the course.
tags: Eroica California 2019 A Vintage Bicycle Festival
videos.curation_reason: High-quality race documentation with detailed first-person experience of the 107-mile Heroic route starting from Cambria at 5AM.

videos.title: Descending Santa Rosa Road at Nova Eroica California on Campagnolo Levante gravel wheels
videos.channel: PezCyclingNews - What's Cool In Road Cycling
videos.description:
The hilight of riding the 2022 Nova Eroica gravel fondo in Cambria CA was (apart from being one of the first to ride Campagnolo's brand new Levante gravel wheelset) was the epic descent off the top of Cypress Mountain along Santa Rosa road and its 22km descent back to the finish.  

I chased Campagnolo's Arjan Hoogesteger off the mountain, and really got to appreciate the control of the wheels, and power and supreme modulation of Campagnolo's Ekar gruppo disc brakes as we dropped down pitches over 12%.   Then he took the strong headwinds head-on as he towed me to the end - much appreciated.

There's more coming on these wheels - but here's my full video of this awesome descent.

Topics
0:00 - Intro & merryment
0:31 - Campagnolo Levante wheels teaser
1:47 - Gravel descent Start
5:28 - Water stop, pavement descent start
22:00 - In the valley and into the wind
videos.curation_reason: Extended first-person descent footage showcasing the epic 22km Santa Rosa Road descent off Cypress Mountain, key terrain feature of the course.

videos.title: Eroica California 2019
videos.description: The 75 mile "Santa Lucia" route of Eroica California April 7, 2019.

quotes.text: The ride starts at 5:00 AM and rolls South through the farms, vineyards, and coastal mountains - this is a 107 mile mixed terrain ride with some pretty tall gearing for the steep gravel roads we'll be climbing.
Look who I found cycling out on the roads of Central Coast of California - the food stops is what you do these things for, olive oil french fries and all.

key_challenges.name: Old Creek Road
key_challenges.description: The big climb of the day, experienced in super foggy conditions with very limited visibility.

key_challenges.name: Santa Rita
key_challenges.description: Referenced as a significant point riders need to get over on the longer routes.

terrain_notes.text: Steep gravel roads that require appropriate gearing. One rider noted their tall gearing wasn't ideal for the steep gravel climbs.

gear_mentions.text: 1960s Bianchi 700cc bike with 32 millimeter wide tires described as not your ultimate all road bike but good enough for the course.
Rider noted having pretty tall gearing that wasn't ideal for climbing steep gravel roads.

race_day_tips.text: Early 5:00 AM start time with riders broken into three groups departing five to ten minutes apart.
They want riders to be at the start by 5:30 for the longer routes.

additional_quotes.text: The food stops is what you do these things for. Olive oil french fries.
Doug from lucky duck bicycles in Oakland stopped by and picked up the Jersey that we accidentally left at home in our rush to get out of town.

rider_intel.search_text: Eroica California features a challenging 107-mile course through Central Coast California starting at 5:00 AM from Cambria. The route includes Old Creek Road as the major climb of the day, often experienced in foggy conditions with limited visibility. Riders face steep gravel roads requiring appropriate gearing, with some noting that tall gearing makes the climbs more difficult. The course passes through Santa Rita as a significant waypoint on longer routes. Multiple food stops provide olive oil french fries and other refreshments. Riders are broken into three groups departing five to ten minutes apart, with a 5:30 AM arrival recommended for longer routes. The vintage cycling event attracts riders on period-appropriate bikes, including steel frames from the 1960s-70s with tire widths around 32mm.

eligibility.notes: CORRECTIONS (2026-07-24, editorial wave 8): status changed from 'unknown' to 'defunct' — high confidence. Last verified edition ran September 15-17, 2023 (7th edition, NOVA on Saturday/Classic on Sunday). The 2024 edition was cancelled with no reason given ('will not be held this year'); no 2025 or 2026 edition has run, and California does not appear on Eroica's official global events calendar. Distance corrected from an uncited '110mi/177km' to the archived final-era Heroic route figure of 107.3mi/172.7km. Elevation (2286m/7500ft) removed — not securely sourced for the Classic route; the 7,500ft figure may reflect cross-route contamination from a 2020 NOVA long-route description (97mi/7,535ft), a different route entirely. Field_size corrected from an unsupported '2,000 participant limit' to a sourced description of actual final-era attendance (county-reported ~1,126 total attendees in 2022; organizer claimed ~1,500 across NOVA+Classic combined; a 2023 projection of ~1,500) — none of these are a field cap. Founded corrected to 2015 (contemporary Bianchi 2015 event-calendar evidence is more authoritative than Eroica's own history page, which currently and likely incorrectly states 2016). Purged one contaminating YouTube entry (video_id JjuEwcbSdAk, 'L'EROICA - BEST RIDE EVER') — its content (Siena, Piazza del Campo, Italian countryside) describes the original L'Eroica in Tuscany, not Eroica California; kept the four videos genuinely about the California event. Added full COVID/discontinuation history and the NOVA Eroica relationship (a genuine co-located spinoff since 2017, not a rebrand of the Classic). See catalog_flags for the successor-event note (Central Coast Classico) and the taxonomy caveat matching eroica-dolomiti.json's precedent.

catalog_flags.status_note: Defunct — last verified edition ran September 15-17, 2023 (7th edition). The 2024 edition was cancelled with no reason given, and no edition has run since; California does not appear on Eroica's official 2025/2026 global calendar. Locally 'replaced' per Central Coast tourism messaging by the independent, unaffiliated Central Coast Classico (inaugural edition April 24-26, 2026) — this is NOT an Eroica-branded continuation or the same organizer, so no duplicate_of relationship applies; recommend a human call on whether/how to cross-reference the unaffiliated successor in the alternatives/final_verdict copy. Also carries the same taxonomy caveat as eroica-dolomiti.json: fondo_rating.discipline is 'sportive' but the actual event (when it ran) was a vintage-bike heritage ride with substantial unpaved content (~20-30% estimated on the Heroic route; verified 22% gravel on the shorter Santa Lucia route) — doesn't cleanly fit a paved-sportive taxonomy or scoring dimensions (road_surface, descent_technicality assume a paved-road competitive event).
